ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for flavoured chocolate drink; SNOMED CT coverage of beverage subtypes is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of flavoured chocolate drink is approximately 250 ml (250 g), equivalent to a single glass or cup as typically consumed.

nutrient_source.title: Flavoured chocolate drink — Singapore HPB Foodo Database (D04020005)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo record D04020005 for flavoured chocolate drink. Saturated fat not reported by HPB; estimated at 0.9 g/100 g based on USDA FoodData Central entry for chocolate-flavoured drink (FDC ID 1104078) as the closest approved secondary source. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, and several micronutrients not reported by HPB and set to -1.

gi_source.title: Chocolate-flavoured milk drink, GI approximately 34–43 (mean ~40) — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for this specific HPB-coded flavoured chocolate drink. GI of 40 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entries for chocolate-flavoured milk-based drinks (GI range 34–43, mean ~40). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (40 × 23.8) / 100 = 9.5, based on 250 g serving with 9.52 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar content (6.85 g/100 g) with low dietary fibre (0.84 g/100 g); sodium is low at 36 mg/100 g but sugar-sweetened beverages independently raise blood pressure risk
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this sugar-sweetened chocolate drink; choose unsweetened cocoa prepared with low-fat milk to obtain cocoa polyphenol benefits without the added sugar load that independently elevates blood pressure risk (AHA 2018).

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage classification (6.85 g sugar/100 g) with low dietary fibre (0.84 g/100 g); liquid sugars are rapidly absorbed and produce acute postprandial glycaemic excursions
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid this flavoured chocolate drink as it qualifies as a sugar-sweetened beverage per ADA criteria; the low fibre content does not attenuate the glycaemic response, and liquid sugar is absorbed faster than equivalent solid carbohydrate.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Added sugar (6.85 g/100 g) and estimated saturated fat (~0.9 g/100 g) in a ready-to-drink format; high iron fortification (28.23 mg/100 g) is atypical and warrants label verification
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this sweetened chocolate drink due to its added sugar content, which contributes to dyslipidaemia and elevated triglycerides; the low sodium (36 mg/100 g) and calcium (131 mg/100 g) are positive attributes but do not offset the cardiovascular risk from regular sugar-sweetened beverage consumption.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar (6.85 g/100 g) promotes pro-inflammatory mediators, offsetting potential anti-inflammatory cocoa polyphenol benefit; dietary fibre is insufficient at 0.84 g/100 g
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should choose unsweetened cocoa powder mixed with water or unsweetened plant milk to access cocoa polyphenols without the sugar-driven inflammatory burden present in this flavoured chocolate drink.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Flavoured chocolate drink is a ready-to-drink or reconstituted chocolate-based beverage made from cocoa powder, sugar, milk solids, and added flavours, typically sold as a liquid or instant powder format. Per 100 g it provides 60 kcal, 1.88 g protein, 1.66 g fat, 9.52 g carbohydrate, 6.85 g sugar, 0.84 g dietary fibre, and 36 mg sodium. It is notably fortified with calcium (131 mg/100 g) and iron (28.23 mg/100 g), the latter being unusually high and indicative of deliberate mineral fortification. The drink has a sweet, mild chocolate flavour with a smooth, slightly creamy texture. It is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) due to its added sugar content, which triggers clinical caution flags for type 2 diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease management. Despite the low sodium and fortified minerals, the sugar load and low fibre content make it unsuitable for individuals managing blood glucose or blood pressure. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 40 (low GI) based on analogous chocolate milk drink data from the University of Sydney GI Database, but the glycemic load per 250 g serving is approximately 9.5, which is clinically meaningful for diabetic meal planning. Cocoa polyphenols present in the drink have theoretical anti-inflammatory properties, but these are offset by the added sugar content. This beverage is commonly consumed as a snack drink or breakfast accompaniment in Singapore and across Southeast Asia.

Can I drink chocolate milk if I have high blood pressure?

Is chocolate drink a sugar-sweetened beverage?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo canonical record D04020005 with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central closest approved equivalent (FDC ID 1104078) and documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous chocolate milk drink entries and documented. All sources are approved under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: Saturated fat (0.9 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for chocolate-flavoured drink (FDC ID 1104078) as HPB does not report saturated fat for this record; this is the closest approved secondary source. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as these values are not reported in the HPB Foodo database for this record and are not available in the USDA FoodData Central closest equivalent with sufficient confidence. The iron value of 28.23 mg/100 g is exceptionally high and strongly suggests deliberate fortification; this value is preserved from the HPB source record. This food is classified as a generic beverage of international origin; no Singaporean-dish multilingual naming requirement applies. GI of 40 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database chocolate milk drink entries (GI range 34–43); no direct GI measurement exists for this specific product. Glycemic load per serving = (40 × 9.52 × 250/100) / 100 = 9.5.
